what_it_does: This file is a terrain-entry ledger record that documents the company-scan artifact: a forensic two-pass local-4B scan of a Windows project tree, the scripts and outputs it produced, its relationships to other entries, and its coverage caveats.
observations: Describes a two-pass scan: pass 1 classified 19,361 source/text files into PROJECTS-LANDSCAPE.md, pass 2 targeted the ElevenLabs Wizard project into DESIGN-DIGEST.md.
Scripts named: scan_projects.py, build_index.py, extract_design.py, synth_design.py.
Used a local 4B model (cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit) at localhost:8000 with 12 worker threads.
Scanned tree path is external on the Windows side: /mnt/c/Users/Workstation001/Documents/Claude/Projects.
Status is unconfirmed/dormant; created and last active on 2026-06-01; no git remote.
Links to [[wizard-run-1]] as direct successor and [[mcp-mining]] as sibling methodology.
Only 4 of 12 files were read; large .jsonl outputs were sized but not parsed.
standouts: Recovered the design of an abandoned project (ElevenLabs Wizard) by scanning 19,361 files with a local 4B model.
Direct successor relationship to wizard-run-1, which later ran the full project-to-product pipeline on the same corpus.
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry YAML frontmatter schema with type, register, status, coverage, relations, and tags.
Uses wiki-style backlinks ([[...]]) for cross-entry relationships.
Includes evidence citations and explicit coverage caveats.
Neutral, descriptive ledger tone without evaluative ranking.
concerns: Status is 'unconfirmed' while the entry makes detailed factual claims.
Coverage caveat states key deliverables and large JSONL files were not fully read or parsed.
'secrets: false' is asserted without showing audit evidence in the file.
The scanned tree is external and not versioned, so reproducibility is not demonstrated here.
notes: A ledger card for a one-day local-4B forensic scan that mapped 19k files and recovered the ElevenLabs Wizard design, with clear lineage to wizard-run-1.
questions: What exactly does DESIGN-DIGEST.md contain, since it was not read in full?
Why is the entry status 'unconfirmed' if the scan outputs are described as finished deliverables?
How accurate were the 4B model's per-file classifications across 19,361 files?
Which specific files or projects besides the Wizard were identified in PROJECTS-LANDSCAPE.md?
Is scan_projects.py still runnable, and do the localhost endpoints/model still exist?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and actual content match: it is a descriptive terrain entry that records what company-scan is, how it works, what it produced, and how it relates to other entries.
apparent_themes: Forensic design recovery from a large, disorganized project tree
Provenance and lineage tracking across related runs
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to preserve a navigable, evidence-based record of a quick but large-scale forensic run so that later work can find the recovered Wizard design and reuse the scanning methodology.
novelty: Using a local 4B model with 12 workers to classify nearly 20,000 files across a Windows project tree and then synthesize a targeted design digest for a specific abandoned project.
contribution: A structured record of the scan's scope, scripts, outputs, model setup, external tree location, and successor/sibling relationships, which a fused system could use to locate the Wizard design recovery and the reusable two-pass extraction pattern.
summary_for_embedding: company-scan is a dormant 2026-06-01 forensic two-pass local-4B scan of a 19,361-file Windows project tree that produced PROJECTS-LANDSCAPE.md and a targeted DESIGN-DIGEST.md for the abandoned ElevenLabs Wizard project.
intention_for_embedding: This entry was created to document the company-scan artifact, its two-pass extraction method, deliverables, and lineage to wizard-run-1 and mcp-mining for future navigation and reuse.
